result.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: Yes, a formal relationship exists via redundancy and information localization, mapped through three correspondences: 1) Redundancy: QD encodes system info in environmental fragments; HQEC encodes bulk info in boundary subregions. 2) Subsystem Structure: QD uses environmental subsystems; HQEC uses boundary subregions (entanglement wedge). 3) Accessibility: QD focuses on pointer state accessibility; HQEC focuses on bulk operator reconstruction. The primary citation gap is the lack of a unified dynamical framework deriving QD decoherence rates from HQEC error-correction properties.
JUSTIFICATION: Correctly maps redundancy, subsystems, and reconstruction while identifying the dynamical gap.
